tasks.name: CI Workflow + Auto-Merge Gate
tasks.description:
# Task 00 — CI Workflow + Auto-Merge Gate

**Phase 0** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** none (entry task)

## Objective
Establish the GitHub Actions CI that gates every task PR and enables safe auto-merge, tuned for an Electron + Node bridge + Python voice monorepo.

## Scope
- GitHub Actions workflow: install, lint, typecheck, and `npm run build` for the Electron/renderer; a Node job for the bridge; a lightweight Python lint/import job for the voice package (skippable until Phase 7 code exists).
- Branch protection on main: PRs require the CI check to pass before merge; configure auto-merge so a green PR merges without manual click.
- Fail fast + clear logs; jobs matrixed by workspace (hud / bridge / voice).
- Document the CI contract in README-DEV.md so every subsequent task knows what 'green' means.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A trivial PR against main is BLOCKED until CI passes, then auto-merges when green.
- CI runs lint + typecheck + build for the HUD, and a bridge job; the voice job no-ops gracefully when there is no Python code yet.
- Branch protection verified active on main.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-00`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Project Scaffold (Electron + Vite + TS)
tasks.description:
# Task 01 — Project Scaffold (Electron + Vite + TS)

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-00

## Objective
Stand up the foundational Electron + Vite + TypeScript project skeleton for the War Room v2 HUD, targeting macOS Apple Silicon.

## Scope
- Electron main + preload + renderer split; contextIsolation on, nodeIntegration off.
- Vite + React 18 + TypeScript renderer; monorepo workspaces for hud / bridge / (voice later).
- ESLint + Prettier + strict tsconfig; scripts: dev, build, lint, typecheck, package.
- A minimal window that loads the renderer (must satisfy task-00 CI).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm run build`, `lint`, `typecheck` pass and CI is green.
- `npm run dev` launches an Electron window rendering a placeholder HUD root.
- Structure documented in README-DEV.md.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-01`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Design System & TNFG Theme Tokens
tasks.description:
# Task 02 — Design System & TNFG Theme Tokens

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-01

## Objective
Establish the visual design system and TNFG theme tokens used by every panel and primitive.

## Scope
- Theme tokens for palette, spacing, typography, z-layers, glow/bloom.
- Reusable primitives: Panel frame, TitleBar, StatPill, DonutBase, TrendLine wrappers.
- Dark near-black base with teal/cyan accents and a red alert state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tokens centralized; no hardcoded colors elsewhere.
- A /dev route renders the primitives for visual QA.
- Crisp at 4K.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-02`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: HUD Aesthetic Primitives (orb, reticles, scanlines, bloom)
tasks.description:
# Task 03 — HUD Aesthetic Primitives (orb, reticles, scanlines, bloom)

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-02

## Objective
Build the signature 'Jarvis-in-TNFG-teal' aesthetic primitives, chiefly the animated Vera Orb.

## Scope
- Animated Orb: energy core + rotating radar rings + ticks; states idle/listening/thinking/speaking/alert/fullscreen.
- Corner reticles, scanlines, grid backdrop, neon bloom overlay.
- Orb exposes a state API the protocol layer can drive later.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Orb renders >=50fps at 4K; six states visually distinct.
- Overlay composable behind panels.
- Screenshot captured.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-03`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Fullscreen Kiosk Window + Crash Auto-Recover
tasks.description:
# Task 04 — Fullscreen Kiosk Window + Crash Auto-Recover

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-01

## Objective
Configure the Electron window for fullscreen kiosk operation with crash auto-recovery.

## Scope
- Fullscreen, no chrome/menu; hide cursor when idle; prevent accidental quit.
- Renderer-gone/crash handler reloads; main-process supervisor.
- Discreet admin/exit hotkey (configurable).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Launches fullscreen, no chrome/cursor.
- Killing the renderer auto-recovers in seconds.
- Admin hotkey reveals exit; normal keys do not quit.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-04`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: App State Store + Config Persistence Layer
tasks.description:
# Task 05 — App State Store + Config Persistence Layer

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-01

## Objective
Create the app-wide state store and a durable local config persistence layer.

## Scope
- State store for panels, layout, orb, connection status.
- Config persistence to disk (layouts, presets, view selection, service-settings pointers).
- Typed schema + migration-safe read/write.

## Acceptance Criteria
- State survives reload; config round-trips to disk.
- Corrupt/missing config falls back to defaults safely.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-05`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-02, task-03

Build the discrete Panel component: header/title bar + swappable content container.

tasks.name: 3x3 Grid Layout Manager (unified virtual canvas)
tasks.description:
# Task 07 — 3x3 Grid Layout Manager (unified virtual canvas)

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-06, task-05

## Objective
Implement the tiling layout manager arranging 9 panels on a unified virtual canvas.

## Scope
- Virtual canvas subdivided into a 3x3 grid; panels occupy cells.
- Default even 3x3, center reserved for the Orb (panel 5).
- Deterministic cell<->pixel mapping; resize-aware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 9 panels render in a clean 3x3; center = Orb.
- Layout data-driven from state.
- Crisp 4K screenshot.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-07`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.description:
# Task 08 — Edit-Layout Mode (drag/resize + snap guides)

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-07

## Objective
Add an Edit-Layout mode letting the user move/resize panels with snapping.

## Scope
- Toggle via hotkey + menu button; drag handles + snap guides.
- Snap to grid cells and canvas edges; exit locks; cancel restores.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can move/resize; panels snap; exiting locks.
- Snap guides visible.
- Cancel restores prior layout.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-08`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

tasks.name: Layout Persistence + Named Presets
tasks.description:
# Task 09 — Layout Persistence + Named Presets

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-08, task-05

## Objective
Persist layouts and support named presets recallable by user and Vera.

## Scope
- Persist current layout; reload on restart.
- Named presets (Briefing, Ops Focus, Single-Screen) save/recall.
- Expose layout.apply(preset) hook.

## Acceptance Criteria
- >=2 presets save/recall.
- Layout persists across restart.
- layout.apply switches presets.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-09`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Multi-Monitor Topology Awareness + Panel Focus/Fullscreen
tasks.description:
# Task 10 — Multi-Monitor Topology Awareness + Panel Focus/Fullscreen

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-07

## Objective
Make the canvas display-topology-aware and add per-panel focus/fullscreen.

## Scope
- Read OS display arrangement (Electron screen API); map canvas across monitors.
- Panels snap to monitor edges; grid extends across added displays.
- panel.focus fullscreens a panel; unfocus/orb return.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canvas ready to span >=2 monitors from topology.
- Any panel can fullscreen and restore.
- Orb can take center stage and return.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-10`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: HUD Protocol Types + Validator (versioned vocabulary)
tasks.description:
# Task 11 — HUD Protocol Types + Validator (versioned vocabulary)

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-01

## Objective
Implement the versioned HUD command protocol as shared TS types + a validator.

## Scope
- Encode envelope + full v1 vocabulary from HUD-PROTOCOL.md.
- Runtime validator (zod): rejects unknown command, bad panel index, unknown view.
- Shared module usable by bridge and HUD.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every v1 command typed.
- Invalid commands rejected with structured hud.error.
- Unit tests for edge cases.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-11`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Bridge Service Skeleton + Gateway WS Client
tasks.description:
# Task 12 — Bridge Service Skeleton + Gateway WS Client

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-11

## Objective
Create the standalone bridge service and its connection to the OpenClaw gateway.

## Scope
- Node/TS service, separate process; connects to gateway ws://127.0.0.1:18789.
- Reconnect/backoff; health endpoint; structured logging.
- Loads config/secrets from the warroom secrets dir; runs independently of the HUD.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bridge connects to the gateway; survives gateway restarts (reconnect).
- Health check reports state.
- Bridge crash does not affect Vera or HUD.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-12`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: HUD<->Bridge WebSocket Channel + Command Router
tasks.description:
# Task 13 — HUD<->Bridge WebSocket Channel + Command Router

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-12, task-07

## Objective
Establish the local WS channel between bridge and HUD and route validated commands.

## Scope
- Bridge exposes a local WS the HUD connects to; HUD sends events (edits, focus, ack/nack).
- Router validates every inbound command (task 11) then dispatches.
- Ack/nack + hud.error on rejection; never silently drop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bridge pushes a command the HUD renders.
- HUD emits events the bridge receives.
- Invalid command -> nack + hud.error.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-13`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Command Handlers (orb/panel/layout/speak) + Mock Data Feed
tasks.description:
# Task 14 — Command Handlers (orb/panel/layout/speak) + Mock Data Feed

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-13, task-10, task-09

## Objective
Implement HUD-side handlers for the full command set, driven by a mock data feed.

## Scope
- Handlers: orb.*, panel.set/clear/focus/unfocus/highlight/update, layout.apply/save/edit, speak(stub), listen.state.
- Mock data provider so panels show content pre-data.
- brief.start stub sequences orb+panels.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every v1 command visibly affects the HUD with mock data.
- brief.start plays a mock briefing.
- Screenshots.

## Delivery

- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

tasks.name: Vera "War Room" Skill (war-room) Authoring
tasks.description:
# Task 15 — Vera "War Room" Skill (war-room) Authoring

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-11

## Objective
Author the additive war-room skill for Vera per VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md (does NOT modify Vera core).

## Scope
- Skill instructions: panel map, briefing choreography, orb-state presence, barge-in re-issue, C6 confirmation, single-source-of-truth, A5 target rule.
- Loaded only in War Room context; dormant otherwise.
- Delivered as an additive skill file; documents Mac-mini install location.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Skill consistent with HUD-PROTOCOL vocabulary.
- Explicitly additive + reversible; no core edits.
- Includes locked ops->email->calendar->flags->handoff order.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-15`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

tasks.name: warroom_emit Tool (bridge-gated) + Registration
tasks.description:
# Task 16 — warroom_emit Tool (bridge-gated) + Registration

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-15, task-12

## Objective
Implement the warroom_emit tool Vera uses to emit validated HUD commands, gated on bridge availability.

## Scope
- Accepts an array of command objects; stamps envelope (protocol/v/id/ts).
- Sends to bridge; returns per-command ack/nack (hud.error on reject).
- Registered additively; inert/hidden when the bridge is unreachable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Vera drives the HUD via warroom_emit.
- Bad commands return hud.error.
- Tool inert with no bridge; Vera normal.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-16`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: End-to-End Vera-Drives-HUD Integration + Resilience
tasks.description:
# Task 17 — End-to-End Vera-Drives-HUD Integration + Resilience

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-16, task-14

## Objective
Wire the full A1-A4 loop and prove resilience: Vera turn -> bridge -> HUD.

## Scope
- A Vera turn drives orb.state/panel.set/panel.clear/panel.focus/layout.apply live.
- Resilience: bridge crash != Vera crash; HUD crash != Vera crash; auto-reconnect.
- Confirm (with task 35) Vera core unmodified except the additive tool/skill.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Live Vera turn manipulates the HUD end to end.
- Each process crashes/restarts without taking down the others.
- Vera's other duties unaffected.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-17`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Vera Core-Integrity Gate (additive/reversible proof)
tasks.description:
# Task 35 — Vera Core-Integrity Gate (additive/reversible proof)

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-16

## Objective
Prove — not merely assert — that installing the War Room tool/skill leaves Vera's core untouched and is cleanly reversible.

## Scope
- Capture a BEFORE snapshot of Vera's install on the Mac mini: agent config, prompt/identity files, skill inventory, tool registry, and file hashes/manifest of the core install dir.
- Install the war-room skill + warroom_emit tool (additive), then capture an AFTER snapshot.
- Diff: the ONLY differences permitted are the added skill file + the added tool registration. Any change to core prompt/identity/config/other-skills FAILS the gate.
- Then run the reversal (remove skill + unregister tool) and capture a THIRD snapshot; it must match the BEFORE snapshot exactly.
- Emit a signed integrity report (before/after/reverted manifests + diff) committed to the repo.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Before->after diff shows ONLY the additive skill + tool; nothing else changed.
- After->reverted returns byte-for-byte to the before state.
- Integrity report committed; this gate blocks Phase 4 completion.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-35`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.name: analyst-01 API Client + Bridge Service-Token Auth (break-proof)
tasks.description:
# Task 20 — analyst-01 API Client + Bridge Service-Token Auth (break-proof)

**Phase 6** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-12

## Objective
Build the analyst-01 API client and the break-proof bridge service-token auth (Option 1).

## Scope
- Client for the analyst-01 endpoints backing the 9 views.
- ADD an additive fail-safe middleware branch on analyst-01: runs only when Bearer==SERVICE_TOKEN from an existing secret file; maps to a read-only warroom-bridge@ viewer; else UNCHANGED fallthrough.
- Constant-time compare + long random secret; guard on secret-file existence (absent => inert).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bridge reads analyst-01 with the service token.
- REGRESSION TESTS pass: existing Google user still authenticates; bad/absent token still 401s; bridge token reads but cannot mutate; deleting the secret reverts to current behavior.
- No change to existing dashboard auth.

- Create feature branch `task-20`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

# Task 28 — Turn-Taking, Barge-In, EOT + Push-to-Talk (on-HUD PTT button)

**Phase 7** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-26, task-27

## Objective
Implement the real-time conversational layer with push-to-talk as the PRIMARY, self-contained input, including a visible on-HUD PTT button.

## Scope
- VAD + end-of-turn detection; barge-in interrupts TTS and adapts.
- Push-to-talk is PRIMARY for v1 (wake word is an additive follow-on in task-29, must not block this).
- REQUIRED on-HUD PTT button: visible, clickable (hold-to-talk + click-to-toggle), active/listening state tied to listen.state + orb; keyboard shortcut as convenience.
- Round-trip tuned toward sub-1.5s; listen.state reflected on HUD (orb + button).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Visible HUD PTT button starts/stops listening (hold and toggle); active state matches orb listening.
- Keyboard PTT works too.
- Barge-in works; latency conversational.
- COMPLETE and usable WITHOUT any wake-word engine (Picovoice not required).

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-28`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

tasks.name: Wake Word (additive; Porcupine/openWakeWord) + C6 Spoken Confirmation
tasks.description:
# Task 29 — Wake Word (additive; Porcupine/openWakeWord) + C6 Spoken Confirmation

**Phase 7** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-28

## Objective
Add the 'Vera' wake word as an ADDITIVE enhancement on top of the working PTT (task-28), and enforce C6 spoken confirmation. Must NOT block earlier phases; Picovoice approval may take days.

## Scope
- C6 (regardless of wake word): read-only executes freely; mutating actions require spoken read-back + explicit 'yes'.
- Wake word (additive): Porcupine custom 'Vera' if a Picovoice AccessKey is present in ~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/picovoice.json; else locally-trained openWakeWord 'Vera'. Config-driven; no rebuild to switch from PTT.
- Coexists with the PTT button; if neither engine configured, ship with wake word disabled + PTT sole activation, cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- C6 enforced.
- With an engine: 'Vera' wakes listening, low false-trigger, PTT still works.
- With NO engine: runs fine on PTT alone, reports wake-word 'not configured' (no failure).
- Engine selection + Picovoice-later path documented.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-29`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Services Configuration Admin Screen (key manager + file spec)
tasks.description:
# Task 30 — Services Configuration Admin Screen (key manager + file spec)

**Phase 8** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-05, task-20

## Objective
Build the in-app admin Services/API-Keys screen and implement the defined secure secrets-file storage.

## Scope
- SECRETS-FILE SPEC (authoritative): directory `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/` (mode 700); one JSON file per service — assemblyai.json, elevenlabs.json, cartesia.json, picovoice.json, analyst01-bridge.json — each `{ "key": "...", "updatedAt": "ISO", "note": "..." }`, file mode 600, owner-only. A single `services.json` index lists which are configured (no secret values). NEVER commit; add to .gitignore.
- Admin screen (behind the admin hotkey) pastes/rotates each key, writing atomically to its file with mode 600; never re-displays a saved secret in plaintext (masked, with a live-probe 'validate' button).
- Bridge + voice processes read keys from these files only.
- Reachable only via the admin hotkey.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Admin can paste/rotate each key without editing shell files; files land at `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/<svc>.json` mode 600.
- Saved keys never re-shown in plaintext; validate button live-probes each.
- Secrets dir gitignored; hidden from normal operation.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-30`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Early macOS Smoke Test on Vera's Mac mini
tasks.description:
# Task 36 — Early macOS Smoke Test on Vera's Mac mini

**Phase 8** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-05

## Objective
Prove the Electron shell actually runs on the real target hardware right after Phase 1, so platform breakage surfaces early — not at packaging.

## Scope
- Deploy the Phase-1 shell build to Vera's Mac mini (M4 Pro, macOS, Apple Silicon) over the `vera` SSH alias.
- Launch it: verify fullscreen kiosk, cursor hide, crash auto-recover, and the admin/exit hotkey all behave on real macOS.
- Probe the macOS-only seams early: Electron `screen` display-topology API returns the real arrangement; app runs arm64 natively; speakers enumerate.
- Record findings; file any platform issues as notes for the relevant later tasks. This is a GATE: if the shell will not run on the Mac mini, later phases stop until fixed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Phase-1 shell launches fullscreen on Vera's actual Mac mini via the `vera` alias.
- screen topology API + arm64 + audio device enumeration verified on real hardware.
- Smoke-test report committed; blocks progression if the shell fails on macOS.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-36`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.description:
# Task 32 — Multi-Monitor Deploy Validation (>=2 displays)

**Phase 8** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-10, task-22

## Objective
Validate multi-monitor spread and snapping on real added displays.

## Scope
- Test with >=2 physical displays; panels spread/snap across monitors.
- Presets that span monitors; per-monitor DPI/scale correctness.
- Document the arrangement setup.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Panels spread/snap across >=2 displays.
- Spanning presets work.
- Crisp on each display.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-32`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

# Task 33 — Packaging, Signing & Vera.app Build

**Phase 8** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-31, task-30, task-36

## Objective
Package and sign the app as Vera.app for clean distribution/auto-launch.

## Scope
- electron-builder mac arm64 build; signing/notarization as feasible.
- Bundle/supervise bridge + voice companion processes.
- Versioned artifact + install/update notes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Signed Vera.app builds and runs on the Mac mini.
- Companion processes start reliably.
- Install documented.

tasks.name: Rollback Runbook — cleanly remove the War Room & restore Vera
tasks.description:
# Task 37 — Rollback Runbook — cleanly remove the War Room & restore Vera

**Phase 8** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-33, task-35

## Objective
Author and dry-run a single documented procedure to fully remove the War Room from the Mac mini and restore Vera to her pre-War-Room state.

## Scope
- A RUNBOOK.md: stop + disable auto-launch/LaunchAgent; quit HUD/bridge/voice processes; remove Vera.app; remove the war-room skill + unregister warroom_emit (reuse task-35 reversal); remove `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/`; remove the analyst-01 fail-safe secret (reverting task-20 middleware to inert).
- Each step: exact command + expected result + verification.
- DRY-RUN the reversal on the Mac mini (or a safe equivalent) and confirm Vera returns to normal (core-integrity snapshot from task 35 matches).
- Include a fast 'demo-safe abort' short-path (kill the room, Vera keeps running) for a live-demo emergency.

## Acceptance Criteria
- RUNBOOK.md fully removes the War Room and restores Vera; verified against the task-35 before-snapshot.
- Includes a one-command 'demo-safe abort' that stops the room without touching Vera.
- Dry-run evidence committed.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-37`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.

tasks.description:
# Task 34 — Production Cutover (Telegram Jon->Zeri) + Handoff Docs

**Phase 8** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2
**Depends on:** task-33, task-32, task-24, task-29, task-37

## Objective
Perform the A5 production cutover and finalize handoff documentation.

## Scope
- Switch the Telegram target from Jon 6492555042 to Zeri's production channel.
- Disable/remove the Jon test target.
- Final KB + Linear updates; operator runbook; hand to Zeri.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zeri's Telegram is the production target; Jon test-target disabled.
- Documented in KB + Linear.
- Handed to Zeri.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-34`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- CI (task-00) must be green before merge.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/*.json` (see task 30 spec), never hardcoded.
